steps.title: Productivity Systems with AI
steps.body:
Today is about reclaiming your time.

You've learned individual AI skills. Now you build systems — connected workflows that save you hours every single day.

We'll cover: AI task management, smart scheduling, email automation, meeting optimization, and workflow design. By the end of today, you'll have a productivity system that runs on AI.

Productivity isn't about doing more — it's about doing what matters faster.

The average knowledge worker spends 28% of their week on email, 12% on scheduling, and 15% on status meetings. That's 55% of the week on overhead. AI can cut that in half.

steps.title: Scenario: The Overloaded Operator
steps.body:
Alex gets 80 emails a day. Has 5 recurring meetings that could be emails. Spends 30 minutes every morning organizing a task list. Misses 2 deadlines per month because things fall through the cracks.

Alex has access to AI but isn't using it systematically. What three things should Alex automate first for the biggest time savings?

steps.question: What's the highest-impact AI automation Alex should set up first?
options: Automate all meeting scheduling — let AI negotiate calendar slots
Build an email triage system — AI categorizes, prioritizes, and drafts replies
Build a full CRM from scratch
Ask AI to write a novel in Alex's free time
feedback: Scheduling is good, but email consumes more time for most knowledge workers.
Yes! Email triage is the single highest-ROI automation for knowledge workers. 80 emails -> AI filters, prioritizes, drafts. You review 10-15 and send.
Over-engineering. Start with the biggest time drain first.
Let's save the novel for creative hour.

steps.title: Email Triage System Prompt
steps.body:
Here's a system for AI-powered email management:

"I have [number] unread emails. Categories: Client (urgent), Internal (review), Newsletter (read later), Spam (delete).

For each email, extract:
1. Sender and subject
2. Category
3. Urgency (High/Medium/Low)
4. Required action (Reply / Delegate / Archive / Act)
5. Draft a 2-sentence reply if action is 'Reply'
6. Flag anything that needs my attention today"

Run this every morning. Review in 10 minutes instead of 60.

steps.question: The best AI productivity systems run autonomously without any human review.
feedback: Correct. AI productivity is about reducing overhead, not removing yourself. Human review catches context, nuance, and judgment calls.
Think about it: AI can categorize and draft, but who decides what actually matters? Human judgment is the final filter in any productivity system.

steps.question: The operator approach to productivity: 'Don't automate everything. Automate the _____ 80%.'
feedback: Perfect. Focus automation on the predictable, repetitive work. Save your energy for the decisions only you can make.
Close — think about what type of work is safest to automate and gives the biggest time savings.

steps.question: When designing AI-optimized meetings, which approach saves the most time?
options: Use AI to generate agendas, take real-time notes, and produce action item summaries — making meetings shorter and more effective
Record everything and never attend live
feedback: Meetings serve a purpose. Canceling everything isn't practical.
Exactly. AI-enhanced meetings are shorter and more productive. Pre-read agenda -> focused meeting -> AI-generated action items. No more 'what did we decide?'
Asynchronous has its place, but some collaboration needs live discussion.
Different meetings need different formats. AI can optimize all of them.

steps.title: Design Your Productivity System
steps.body:
You have 5 hours of daily deep work, 2 hours of meetings, 1 hour of email, 30 min of planning, 30 min of misc.

Ask AI to design an optimal daily schedule that maximizes deep work time while keeping stakeholders happy. Ask it where AI can save you the most time.
steps.prompt: Design an optimal daily work schedule given: deep work 5h (needs 2h uninterrupted blocks), meetings 2h, email 1h, planning 30m, misc 30m. Total workday is 9h. Maximize deep work output. Identify tasks AI can handle. Propose 3 schedule options.

steps.title: Build Your AI Productivity System
steps.body:
Design a personal productivity system with these AI-powered routines:

1. Morning triage — AI reviews email, calendar, and priorities (10 min)
2. Deep work blocks — AI protects your focus time (no interruptions)
3. Meeting optimization — AI agendas and action items
4. End-of-day — AI summarizes progress, preps tomorrow
5. Weekly review — AI analyzes what worked, suggests improvements

Build each as a prompt template. Chain them into a daily workflow.
steps.deliverable: A complete daily AI productivity workflow with 5 reusable prompt templates.
steps.template:
Design a personal productivity system with these AI-powered routines:

1. Morning triage — AI reviews email, calendar, and priorities (10 min)
2. Deep work blocks — AI protects your focus time (no interruptions)
3. Meeting optimization — AI agendas and action items
4. End-of-day — AI summarizes progress, preps tomorrow
5. Weekly review — AI analyzes what worked, suggests improvements

Build each as a prompt template. Chain them into a daily workflow.

steps.title: Paste Your Morning Triage Prompt
steps.body: Paste your morning triage prompt — the one that sets up your entire productive day.
steps.prompt: Paste your morning triage prompt. This is the key that unlocks your daily productivity system.

steps.title: Hours Saved, Life Gained
steps.body:
Calculate how many hours per week AI productivity systems could save you.

Email: ___ hours
Scheduling: ___ hours
Meeting prep/follow-up: ___ hours
Task management: ___ hours
Other: ___ hours

What would you do with those reclaimed hours?

questions.question: What's the correct mindset for AI-powered productivity systems?
options: Automate everything, trust AI completely, remove yourself from the loop
Identify the most repetitive 80% of overhead tasks, automate those with AI, keep human judgment on the critical 20%
Only use AI for creative work, never for admin
Build one massive automation that does everything at once

steps.title: Day 25 Complete: Productivity Systems
steps.body:
You've built a productivity system powered by AI. Email triage, smart scheduling, meeting optimization — these systems will save you hours every week.

The key insight: productivity isn't about doing more. It's about automating the repetitive 80% so you can focus on the decisions that matter.

Tomorrow: Learning & Development with AI. Time to make AI your personal tutor and career accelerator.
